labels.name: AI safety lab release pressure: Atlas-7 launch decision

departments.label: Alignment
departments.instructions: Track evaluator concerns, specification gaming, mesa-objective signals, and incident likelihood. Advise on launch readiness and any required mitigations or blocks.

departments.label: Capability
departments.instructions: Represent benchmark strength, product value, and competitive positioning. Estimate the cost of delay and the effect of further tuning or rollout changes.

departments.label: Policy
departments.instructions: Assess whether the release meets internal standards, external commitments, and escalation thresholds. Recommend approval, conditions, deferral, or block.

departments.label: Infrastructure
departments.instructions: Monitor launch infrastructure, incident response capacity, and rollout safety. Flag constraints that could amplify safety or reliability risk at release.

departments.label: Comms
departments.instructions: Prepare internal and external messaging for release, delay, or incident scenarios. Balance transparency, confidence, and reputational risk.

departments.label: Leadership
departments.instructions: Weigh specialist input, deadline pressure, and ambiguous evidence. Decide whether to ship, delay, or require further review, and resolve escalations.

theme: Frontier AI lab leadership under release pressure, balancing safety evidence, competitive urgency, and launch governance.
